EMAIL TEMPLATES 5 Essential Email Templates for Recruiters: From Intake to Follow-Up Siddhartha Gunti March 31, 2025 Effective communication between recruiters and hiring managers is key to a smooth hiring process. These email templates will help streamline your intake meetings and follow-ups. From initial requests to post-meeting summaries, we've got you covered. Use these templates as a starting point and customize them to fit your organization's tone and needs. Table of contents Crafting a Professional and Effective Email Template Initial Intake Request Email Gentle Reminder Email (Intake Meeting) Intake Meeting Confirmation Email Post-Intake Meeting Summary Email Revised Requirements Request Email Intake Meeting Cancellation Email Download Request an Intake Meeting: From Recruiter to Hiring Manager Email Templates in multiple formats Wrapping Up Crafting a Professional and Effective Email Template Creating a professional email template for intake meetings is important for recruiters. It ensures clear communication and sets the tone for collaboration. A well-structured email can significantly enhance your candidate experience. Start with a concise subject line that clearly states the purpose of the email. This helps the hiring manager understand the email's intent at a glance. A straightforward subject line can improve open rates and engagement. Begin the email with a polite greeting and a brief introduction of yourself. Mention your role and the purpose of the email to establish context. This sets a courteous tone and provides clarity. Clearly state the objective of the intake meeting. Explain the topics you wish to discuss, such as job requirements and candidate profiles. This helps the hiring manager prepare for a productive meeting. Include a call to action by suggesting a few dates and times for the meeting. Encourage the hiring manager to confirm or propose alternatives. This demonstrates your flexibility and eagerness to collaborate. End the email with a polite closing and your contact information. Offer to provide additional details if needed. A courteous closing leaves a positive impression and opens the door for further communication. Initial Intake Request Email This template is used to initiate the intake meeting request with the hiring manager. It sets the stage for a productive discussion about the role and candidate requirements, similar to how you approach a job requisition. Subject: Let's Schedule an Intake Meeting for [Job Title] Hi [Hiring Manager's Name], I hope this message finds you well. I'm reaching out to schedule an intake meeting to discuss the [Job Title] position. This meeting will help us align on the role's requirements and the ideal candidate profile. Here are a few points we might cover: Key responsibilities and goals for the position Required skills and experience Team dynamics and company culture Timeline for the hiring process Could you please let me know your available times this week? Looking forward to collaborating to find the perfect fit for your team. Best regards, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information] Gentle Reminder Email (Intake Meeting) Use this template to follow up if the hiring manager hasn't responded to your initial request. It's a polite way to nudge them and keep the hiring process moving, ensuring you meet your time-to-fill goals. Subject: Gentle Reminder: Intake Meeting Request Hi [Hiring Manager's Name], I hope this message finds you well. I wanted to follow up on my previous request to schedule an intake meeting for the [Job Title] position. Your insights are important to us as we aim to find the best fit for your team. Could you let me know your available times this week or next? We're eager to get started and value your input in the process. Thank you for your attention to this. Looking forward to your response. Best regards, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information] Intake Meeting Confirmation Email Once the hiring manager agrees to a meeting, send this confirmation email. This template solidifies the date, time, and purpose of the meeting, ensuring everyone is on the same page. Subject: Confirmation of Intake Meeting Hi [Hiring Manager's Name], Thank you for agreeing to meet and discuss the upcoming role. I’m looking forward to our intake meeting to align on the requirements and expectations. Here are the details for our meeting: Date: [Insert Date] Time: [Insert Time] Location/Platform: [Insert Location/Platform] The goal of this meeting is to understand the key aspects of the position and any specific qualifications or skills you are looking for in candidates. Please let me know if there’s anything specific you would like to add to the agenda or if there are any changes to the schedule. Looking forward to our conversation! Best regards, [Your Name][Your Position][Your Contact Information] Post-Intake Meeting Summary Email After the intake meeting, send this email to summarize the key discussion points and agreed-upon next steps. This ensures alignment and provides a reference point for both you and the hiring manager, which is a part of the selection process. Subject: Post-Intake Meeting Summary Hi [Hiring Manager's Name], Thank you for taking the time to meet with me earlier. Here's a quick summary of our discussion: Role Overview: We discussed the key responsibilities and expectations for the [Job Title] role. Candidate Profile: You highlighted the must-have skills and experience, including [list any specific skills]. Timeline: We agreed on the following timeline for the recruitment process: [insert timeline details]. Next Steps: I'll start sourcing candidates based on our discussion. We'll reconvene on [insert date] to review initial candidates. Please let me know if there's anything I missed or if you have further thoughts. Looking forward to working together to find the right fit for your team! Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information] Revised Requirements Request Email Sometimes, after initial candidate reviews, the hiring manager might want to adjust the requirements. This template helps you formally request and document those changes, ensuring you're targeting the right talent pool. Subject: Request for Updated Requirements for [Job Title] Role Hi [Hiring Manager's Name], I hope this message finds you well. After reviewing the initial batch of candidates for the [Job Title] position, it seems we might need to revisit the job requirements to better align with the ideal candidate profile. Could you please let me know a convenient time for us to discuss any adjustments or updates you think are necessary? This will help us focus our search and ensure we're attracting the most suitable candidates. Looking forward to your insights. Best regards, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information] Intake Meeting Cancellation Email If either you or the hiring manager needs to cancel the intake meeting, use this template to inform the other party professionally. Remember to propose alternative times to reschedule, keeping the hiring timeline on track. Subject: Intake Meeting Cancellation - [Job Title] Hi [Hiring Manager Name], I'm writing to inform you that I need to reschedule our intake meeting for the [Job Title] role, originally scheduled for [Original Date and Time]. [ Briefly explain the reason for cancellation. Keep it concise and professional. For example: "Due to an unforeseen scheduling conflict," or "I need to attend to an urgent matter."] I apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. To keep things moving, I'd like to propose a few alternative times for the intake meeting: [Date] at [Time] [Date] at [Time] [Date] at [Time] Please let me know which of these times works best for you. If none of these options suit your schedule, please suggest an alternative, and I'll do my best to accommodate. Thanks for your understanding, and I look forward to our conversation. Best regards, [Your Name] [Your Title] Adaface Download Request an Intake Meeting: From Recruiter to Hiring Manager Email Templates in multiple formats Download image Download PDF Wrapping Up Crafting effective emails for intake meetings is a key skill for recruiters. By using these templates, you can streamline communication and ensure all parties are on the same page. Remember, clear communication not only saves time but also enhances the recruitment process. Tailor these templates to fit your unique needs and watch your efficiency soar. Request an Intake Meeting: From Recruiter to Hiring Manager Email Template FAQs How do I politely follow up on an intake meeting request? Wait 3-5 business days after your initial email Send a brief, friendly reminder Restate the purpose and importance of the meeting Offer flexible scheduling options What should I include in a post-intake meeting summary? Key points discussed during the meeting Agreed-upon job requirements and qualifications Next steps and action items Timeline for the hiring process Any questions that need further clarification How can I make my intake request emails more effective? Clearly state the purpose of the meeting Highlight the benefits of a thorough intake Provide a brief agenda or list of topics to cover Suggest specific time slots for the meeting Keep the email concise and easy to read Should I use AI to write my recruitment emails? AI can be a helpful starting point Always review and personalize AI-generated content Ensure the tone matches your company culture Use AI for inspiration, not as a replacement for human touch How often should I update the hiring manager after the intake meeting? Provide weekly updates on the recruitment progress Send immediate updates for significant developments Schedule brief check-ins if the process extends beyond expected timelines Adjust frequency based on the hiring manager's preferences 40 min skill tests.
